name: Acarbose improved aged-mouse physical function and cardiac/lipid aging measures
summary: Acarbose improved several aged-mouse healthspan measures, but the evidence remains preclinical and partly sex-dependent.
statement: In aging genetically heterogeneous mice, acarbose improved balance/coordination, rotarod endurance, and grip strength, attenuated male-specific cardiac hypertrophy, and shifted cardiac and liver lipid biology.
caveats: The functional and cardiac evidence is mouse-only.
Lipid and pathway changes are mechanistic context rather than direct human outcomes.
